items.markdown:
**Anomaly detection** (often called outlier detection) is `the process of identifying data points, events, or behaviors that significantly deviate from the norm` . By automating the analysis of large datasets, it acts as an early warning system to catch errors, security threats, and critical incidents.[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/anomaly-detection) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/anomaly-detection)[[2]](https://www.vmware.com/topics/anomaly-detection)[[3]](https://www.crowdstrike.com/en-us/cybersecurity-101/next-gen-siem/anomaly-detection/)

Why It Matters

Because data volumes are too massive for manual tracking, organizations rely on artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) to spot hidden problems. Benefits include:[](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/anomaly-detection) [[1]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/anomaly-detection)

- **Cost Savings:** Prevents revenue loss by stopping fraud before it occurs.
- **Downtime Prevention:** Detects minor performance changes in machinery or IT systems before a catastrophic failure happens.
- **Risk Mitigation:** Alerts teams to unauthorized access or security breaches in real time.[](https://www.crowdstrike.com/en-us/cybersecurity-101/next-gen-siem/anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.crowdstrike.com/en-us/cybersecurity-101/next-gen-siem/anomaly-detection/)[[2]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/anomaly-detection/)[[3]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)[[4]](https://www.centreon.com/glossary/definition-anomaly-detection/)[[5]](https://datadome.co/learning-center/ai-fraud-detection/)

Common Use Cases

- **Fraud Detection:** Flags irregular credit card transactions, suspicious insurance claims, or rapid account activity that could indicate hackers.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)
- **Cybersecurity:** Identifies unusual network traffic or abnormal login patterns that signal a cyberattack or malware.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)
- **Healthcare:** Monitors deviations in patient vital signs (e.g., heart rate) or wearable device data to catch underlying medical issues early.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)
- **Industrial Monitoring:** Tracks sensor data from manufacturing equipment to identify parts showing early signs of wear.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)[[2]](https://www.meegle.com/en_us/topics/anomaly-detection/anomaly-detection-in-artificial-intelligence)[[3]](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/anomaly-detection/)[[4]](https://www.snowflake.com/en/fundamentals/anomaly-detection/)

How It Works Modern anomaly detection typically employs one of three machine learning approaches:[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)

1. **Supervised:** The model is trained using historical datasets containing previously labeled data (both "normal" and "anomalous") to recognize specific types of bad behavior.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)
2. **Unsupervised:** The model analyzes unlabeled data, learning what "normal" behavior looks like over time and simply flagging anything that falls outside that baseline.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)[[2]](https://www.vmware.com/topics/anomaly-detection)
3. **Semi-Supervised:** The model builds a baseline exclusively from normal data and alerts administrators whenever it encounters data that breaks that established pattern.[](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/) [[1]](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/computer-networks/what-is-anomaly-detection/)[[2]](https://rtslabs.com/ai-anomaly-detection/)

For more information on the algorithms and tools used in this space, visit the [IBM Anomaly Detection Guide](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/anomaly-detection) or explore the [AWS Anomaly Detection Overview](https://aws.amazon.com/what-is/anomaly-detection/).

items.title: What does an Anomaly Detector do?

expanded_element.title: What is Anomaly Detection? - AWS
expanded_element.description: What does anomaly detection do? Anomaly detection identifies suspicious activity that falls outside of your established normal patterns of behavior. A solution protects your system in real-time from instances that could result in significant financial losses, data breaches, and other harmful events.

items.title: What is the meaning of anomalies identified?

expanded_element.title: What Is Anomaly Detection? Examples, Techniques & Solutions
expanded_element.description: Anomaly detection identifies unusual patterns or outliers in data that deviate from expected behavior, enabling proactive detection of issues like fraud, security threats, or equipment failures.

expanded_element.title: Anomaly detection powered by AI - Dynatrace
expanded_element.description: Anomaly detection is a technique that uses AI to identify abnormal behavior as compared to an established pattern. Anything that deviates from an established baseline pattern is considered an anomaly.
